title: Make all expressions derive from AstNodeMath.
body:
This gets rid of the Statement/Expression ambiguity previously peresent, and now all expressions are AstNodeMath. If it was me, AstNodeMath would be called AstNodeExpr as that's what compiler engineers would expect, but I'm ok not to rename it unless it's desirable. Please let me know if you have any objections.

TODO:
- [x] Describe in internals docs

---

Now every expression (i.e.: those AstNodes that represent a [possibly void] value, with value being interpreted in a very general sense) has AstNodeMath as a super class. This necessitates the introduction of an AstStmtMath, which represents an expression in statement position, e.g : 'foo();' would be represented as AstStmtMath(AstCCall(foo)). In exchange we can get rid of isStatement() in AstNodeStmt, which now really always represent a statement

Peak memory consumption and verilation speed are not measurably changed.

Partial step towards #3420
